english: mód (mod) to-day. — 4. an equal, a match; a companion, associate, W. *yá-do* (ya do) fellow, yoke-fellow, mate, comrade, consort; do-zla (do zla) 1. id. Mil.; 2. party in a lawsuit(?); *dó-da ṗan-tsún zib ċẹ́-pa* (do bdag phan tshun zhib byas pa) Cs. seems to mean: carefully to investigate (the right of) both parties; do - med (do med) unequalled, matchless; *dho-med zaṅ-po* (do med bzang po), C., W.

importance, weight; important, weighty C., W.; *dho-ghál mi jhe'-pa* (do gal mi byed pa) C., *do-gál mi ċó-ċe* (do gal mi bco ce) W., to treat lightly, to make light of, to slight; *di tsíg-po dho-ghál mi dug* ('di tshig po do gal mi 'dug) C., this word is unimportant, of no consequence Cs.; do-gál-ċan (do gal can) important, of consequence Cs.

commission, charge, superintendence; *dho-dhám jhe'-pa* (do dam byed pa) C., *do-dám kúr-ċe* (do dam khur ce) W., to have the superintendence, direction, or charge of a business, to have the keeping of a thing; do-dám-pa (do dam pa) 1. a commissioned, authorized person, overseer etc.; 2. bishop Chr. Prot.

a load, for a beast of burden, cf. dos (dos); *do-góm* (do gom) W. saddle-cloth, housing; do-lógs (do logs) the load on one side of a sumpter-horse, half a load, do ya-gċig (do ya gcig); do-nón-pa (do non pa) the equalizing of the load, by increasing or lessening it on one of the sides.

1. Jerusalem artichoke Sik. — 2. secondary form of sdó-ba (sdo ba) c. accus., to be a match for, to be equal in strength etc., to cope with Mil.; *śrog daṅ dhón-da re* (srog dang bsdon da re) C. his life is at stake (da?) (da).
Hind. a thick shawl or wrapper W.
Cs. n. of an ornament hanging down from the shoulders; Schr. mu-tig-gi do-śàl (mu tig gi do shal) pearl-necklace; Mil. id.

col. an auxiliary vb., acc. to Lamas of W. and C. = rtóg-pa (rtog pa), but of different pronunciation (W. *dog* (dog), C. *dhog* (dhog)). It seems to correspond to the expressions: as far as I know, as much as you know, to your knowledge etc. So a person may be asked: *yóg - mo me bar dóg - ga(m)* (yog mo me 'bar dog gam) has your maid-servant, for what you know, lighted a fire? whilst, if the servant herself were asked, the question could only be: *me bar-ra(m)* (me 'bar ram), or *bar tsar-ra(m)* ('bar tshar ram).

sbst., in B. mostly dóg-pa (dog pa), 1. bundle, clew, skein, e.g. of wool, weighing about two pounds, as much as one can hold conveniently with the hand or twist round it (lag-dóg) (lag dog). — 2. capsule, àr-dza-kai ('ar dza kai) of the cotton plant. — 3. ear of corn Lex.; Col. more in use: dog-dóg (dog dog) a larger piece; ká-ra dog-dóg (ka ra dog dog), lump-sugar (opp. to ground sugar); clod, clump, lump, loaf, *dog-dóg ċó-ċe* (dog dog bco ce) W. to form loaves; or in general: to press, to press together, to crush, to crumple; a piece of wood, a log W. (differing from rdog (rdog)); *dúg-ga-dog-gé* (dug ga dog ge) Ld. broken in pieces, e.g. k'a-ra (kha ra).

1. v. dog (dog) sbst. — 2. adj. and sbst., narrow, narrowness; dóg-po (dog po), dóg-mo (dog mo) adj.; dóg-pai gnas-las tar-ba (dog pai gnas las thar ba) Wdn.; fig. śin-tú dóg-par gyúr-to (shin tu dog par gyur to) they were kept within narrow bounds Glr.; *ṭim dhóg-po* (khrims dog po) C. strict administration of justice.
an iron pan with a handle C., W.

1. vb., to fear, to be afraid of, to apprehend, gen. with the root of the pf tense, which in earlier writings is placed in the instrum. case: nyés-pa byúṅ-yis mi dogs (nyes pa byung yis mi dogs) Dzl.; whereas Glr.: ser byuṅ dógs-pai dús-su (ser byung dogs pai dus su) (fearing) when a hail-storm is threatening; Tar. 188, 9: rgyal-srid ma zin-gyi(s) dógs-te (rgyal srid ma zin gyis dogs te) being afraid (the prince) might not be able to govern; ma zin dogs-pas (ma zin dogs pas) Glr. fearing lest he should not finish
